topNews.title: [nexu-io/open-design] 🎨 Local-first open replica of Anthropic's Claude Design. ⚡ 19 Skills · ✨ 71 brand-grade Design Systems · 🖼️ sandboxed preview · 📦 HTML/PDF/PPTX export. 🤖 Runs on Claude Code / Codex / Cursor / Gemini CLI / OpenCode / Qwen.
topNews.rawTextSnippet: 🎨 Local-first open replica of Anthropic's Claude Design. ⚡ 19 Skills · ✨ 71 brand-grade Design Systems · 🖼️ sandboxed preview · 📦 HTML/PDF/PPTX export. 🤖 Runs on Claude Code / Codex / Cursor / Gemini CLI / OpenCode / Qwen.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: A local-first open replica of Anthropic's Claude Design was launched, featuring 19 skills and 71 design systems.  
• Why it matters: This tool enhances accessibility and usability for designers by allowing local operation and versatile export options.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this open-source design system to streamline their design processes and reduce reliance on proprietary software.

topNews.title: OpenAI CEO's Identity Verification Company Announced Fake Bruno Mars Partnership

topNews.summary:
• What happened: OpenAI's CEO's identity verification company falsely claimed a partnership with Bruno Mars.  
• Why it matters: This incident raises concerns about credibility and trust in identity verification services.  
• Business opportunity: There is a demand for robust verification processes to prevent misinformation and enhance brand integrity.

topNews.title: OpenAI Misses Key Revenue, User Targets in High-Stakes Sprint Toward IPO
topNews.rawTextSnippet: OpenAI Misses Key Revenue, User Targets in High-Stakes Sprint Toward IPO
topNews.summary:
• What happened: OpenAI failed to meet crucial revenue and user growth targets ahead of its IPO.  
• Why it matters: This shortfall raises concerns about the company's market readiness and investor confidence.  
• Business opportunity: There may be potential for competitors to capitalize on OpenAI's challenges by attracting users and investors.

topNews.title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its ‘mind-reading’ tech for consumer wearables
topNews.rawTextSnippet: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech—a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer applications.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Neurable, a BCI startup, aims to license its non-invasive mind-reading technology for consumer wearables.  
• Why it matters: This innovation could revolutionize how users interact with devices, enhancing accessibility and user experience.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can explore partnerships to integrate this technology into various consumer products, tapping into a growing market for advanced wearables.

topNews.title: Red Hat’s OpenClaw maintainer just made enterprise Claw deployments a lot safer
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Tank OS puts OpenClaw AI agents into a container that let's it run reliably and more safely, especially for those running fleets of them.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Red Hat's OpenClaw maintainer improved enterprise Claw deployments by introducing Tank OS for enhanced safety.  
• Why it matters: This advancement ensures more reliable operation for businesses utilizing fleets of OpenClaw AI agents.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this safer deployment to optimize AI operations and reduce risks in their infrastructure.

topNews.title: Show HN: LavinMQ, an open-source message broker written in Crystal
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Hi HN. I'm Carl, creator of LavinMQ. LavinMQ is an open-source message broker. AMQP 0-9-1, MQTT, HTTP and streaming. Single binary, minimal resource use. If you know RabbitMQ, it's that (but running on a fraction of hardware). We're the team behind CloudAMQP where we've hosted Ra…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: LavinMQ, an open-source message broker, was introduced by its creator, Carl.  
• Why it matters: It offers a lightweight alternative to existing brokers like RabbitMQ, requiring minimal resources.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage LavinMQ for cost-effective messaging solutions in resource-constrained environments.

topNews.title: Show HN: OSS Agent I built topped the TerminalBench on Gemini-3-flash-preview
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Scored 65.2% vs google's official 47.8%, and the existing top closed source model Junie CLI's 64.3%. Since there are a lot of reports of deliberate cheating on TerminalBench 2.0 lately ( https://debugml.github.io/cheating-agents/ ), I would like to also clarify a few th…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: An open-source agent outperformed Google's model and a leading closed-source competitor in TerminalBench testing.  
• Why it matters: This achievement highlights the potential of open-source solutions in competitive AI benchmarks, challenging proprietary models.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this open-source agent to enhance their AI capabilities and reduce reliance on costly closed-source alternatives.

funding.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
funding.rawTextSnippet: After a yearslong legal feud, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are heading to trial this week in Northern California in a case that could have sweeping consequences. Ahead of OpenAI’s highly anticipated IPO, the court could rule on whether the company is allowed to exist as a for-profit enterpris…

dailyBrief: In today's AI landscape, the open-source community is buzzing with the launch of nexu-io's local-first replica of Anthropic's Claude Design, featuring a robust suite of 19 skills and 71 design systems, which could significantly enhance design workflows for businesses. Meanwhile, OpenAI faces scrutiny as its CEO's identity verification venture mistakenly announced a partnership with Bruno Mars, raising questions about credibility in the rapidly evolving AI sector. Compounding these challenges, OpenAI has reportedly missed key revenue and user targets in its aggressive push toward an IPO, indicating potential volatility for investors. On a more promising note, BCI startup Neurable is seeking to license its innovative 'mind-reading' technology for consumer wearables, potentially opening new revenue streams in the burgeoning market for neurotechnology. Lastly, Red Hat’s OpenClaw maintainer has improved enterprise deployment safety, a critical factor for businesses relying on robust AI solutions.
